sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचकुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम् । अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्तिकरमर्जुन ॥
hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान् बोले   हे अर्जुन इस विषम अवसरपर तुम्हें यह कायरता कहाँसे प्राप्त हुई, जिसका कि श्रेष्ठ पुरुष सेवन नहीं करते, जो स्वर्गको देनेवाली नहीं है और कीर्ति करनेवाली भी नहीं है ॥
englishTranslation: Krishna said: Arjuna, where has this delusion come from in this crisis? Noble people do not follow it. It does not lead upward, and it brings disgrace.
essence: Delusion has no place in a decisive moment.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna's collapse is called out as misplaced weakness. It is not the response of a noble person, and it only lowers him.
simpleInsight: A hard moment does not excuse surrendering to confusion. You can notice weakness without obeying it.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra field, Arjuna has dropped into grief and hesitation while the armies wait. Krishna answers sharply, naming the paralysis as out of place in this decisive moment. This verse begins the push to restore action.
modernRelevance: You are about to send the message that changes everything, and your hand freezes above the screen. The mind calls it caution; Krishna calls it collapse.
translationLiteral: श्री भगवान् उवाच (the divine said) / कुतः (from where) / त्वा (to you) / कश्मलम् (delusion) / इदम् (this) / विषमे (in this crisis) / समुपस्थितम् (has arisen) / अनार्य-जुष्टम् (not followed by noble people) / अस्वर्ग्यम् (not leading upward) / अकीर्ति-करम् (bringing disgrace) / अर्जुन (Arjuna)
insight: It teaches you to interrupt panic before it becomes your identity.
